title: Bioengineering
recommendedTrackSummary: AST-2 is the current closest Green River transfer path for this degree because it matches 13 of the 21 degree-specific Green River classes currently tracked for this major.
whyThisTrack: AST-2 has the strongest direct overlap with the current degree-specific Green River class list, including CHEM& 161, MATH& 151, CHEM& 162, MATH& 152, plus 9 more.
The matched track title also aligns with Bioengineering.

This auto-match compares every hardcoded course in the current Green River transfer tracks against the major's tracked Green River classes and keeps the track with the highest concrete course overlap.
Use the remaining major-specific checklist items to add the classes that AST-2 does not cover by itself.

beforeEnrollmentChecklist.title: BIOEN 401 or BIOEN 402 or BIOEN 404 or BIOEN 405

requirementGroup.label: BIOEN 401 or BIOEN 402 or BIOEN 404 or BIOEN 405
requirementGroup.sourceHeading: The capstone project should relate to the chosen senior emphasis. Students may choose a path that includes integrated design and research (BIOEN 401 & 402), or a path that includes a small-group design project (BIOEN 404 & 405) plus additional engineering elective credit as needed. If the group/team design project path is chosen, extracurricular research is encouraged but not required.
requirementGroup.sourceRowText: The capstone project should relate to the chosen senior emphasis. Students may choose a path that includes integrated design and research (BIOEN 401 & 402), or a path that includes a small-group design project (BIOEN 404 & 405) plus additional engineering elective credit as needed. If the group/team design project path is chosen, extracurricular research is encouraged but not required.

requirementGroups.sourceHeading: The capstone project should relate to the chosen senior emphasis. Students may choose a path that includes integrated design and research (BIOEN 401 & 402), or a path that includes a small-group design project (BIOEN 404 & 405) plus additional engineering elective credit as needed. If the group/team design project path is chosen, extracurricular research is encouraged but not required.
